title: CUA-Suite: Massive Human-annotated Video Demonstrations for Computer-Use Agents
task: Providing large-scale video demonstrations for computer-use agents
key_results: VideoCUA: ~10K tasks, 55 hours continuous video, 6M frames; GroundCUA: 56K screenshots, 3.6M UI annotations; current models ~60% failure rate
comments: CUA-Suite is a major resource contribution; 55 hours of continuous video is transformative for the field but still modest compared to internet scale.

model_io.model: Foundation action models (unspecified)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Continuous video with temporal dynamics is critical for scaling computer-use agents beyond sparse screenshots
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via dataset design; high failure rate on professional apps suggests this is a key bottleneck

title: EVA: Efficient Reinforcement Learning for End-to-End Video Agent
task: Efficient video understanding via planning-before-perception
key_results: 6-12% improvement over MLLM baselines, 1-3% gain over prior adaptive methods on six video benchmarks
comments: Three-stage pipeline (SFT→KTO→GRPO) is novel but adds complexity; unclear how much improvement comes from each stage versus the planning-first strategy.

model_io.model: EVA (MLLM-based agent with RL)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Planning-before-perception enables more efficient video understanding than perception-first strategies
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via 6-12% improvement over perception-first baselines

title: T-MAP: Red-Teaming LLM Agents with Trajectory-aware Evolutionary Search
task: Red-teaming LLM agents via trajectory-aware evolutionary search
key_results: Substantially higher attack realization rate (ARR) than baselines; effective against GPT-5.2, Gemini-3-Pro, Qwen3.5, GLM-5
comments: First work to focus on multi-step tool execution vulnerabilities in MCP; methodology is sound but exact ARR numbers not disclosed in abstract.

model_io.model: T-MAP (evolutionary search optimizer)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trajectory-aware search better discovers agent vulnerabilities than prior text-only red-teaming
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; T-MAP outperforms baselines in ARR across multiple frontier models

title: UI-Voyager: A Self-Evolving GUI Agent Learning via Failed Experience
task: Mobile GUI automation via self-evolving agent with failed experience learning
key_results: 4B model achieves 81.0% Pass@1 success rate on AndroidWorld, exceeding human performance
comments: GRSD's identification of fork points is clever but requires group rollouts, adding computational cost; strong empirical results warrant attention.

model_io.model: UI-Voyager (MLLM-based mobile agent)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-evolution via failed trajectory correction outperforms sparse reward learning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; RFT+GRSD achieves 81% success, surpassing baselines and human performance

title: Why Does Self-Distillation (Sometimes) Degrade the Reasoning Capability of LLMs?
task: Understanding self-distillation degradation in mathematical reasoning
key_results: Performance drops up to 40% in mathematical reasoning; suppression of epistemic verbalization identified as root cause
comments: Well-designed controlled experiments; finding is important but applies primarily to reasoning tasks—effectiveness may differ for other domains.

model_io.model: Self-distillation framework (Qwen3-8B, DeepSeek-Distill-Qwen-7B, Olmo3-7B-Instruct)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Conditioning teacher on rich context suppresses uncertainty, harming OOD reasoning performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; degradation up to 40% observed; uncertainty expression crucial for robust reasoning

title: GameplayQA: A Benchmarking Framework for Decision-Dense POV-Synced Multi-Video Understanding of 3D Virtual Agents
task: Multi-video understanding and reasoning for autonomous agents in 3D environments
key_results: Benchmarks MLLMs with 2.4K diagnostic QA pairs at 1.22 labels/second; reveals substantial gap from human performance
comments: GameplayQA's dense annotation and triadic decomposition (Self/Other/World) are valuable; benchmark is well-structured but limited to gameplay settings.

model_io.model: Frontier MLLMs evaluated (not specified which)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current MLLMs struggle with temporal grounding, agent attribution, and decision density in multi-agent environments
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; common failure modes identified through diagnostic QA evaluation

title: When Models Judge Themselves: Unsupervised Self-Evolution for Multimodal Reasoning
task: Unsupervised self-evolution for multimodal reasoning without human annotations
key_results: Consistent improvements on five mathematical reasoning benchmarks using only self-consistency signal and GRPO
comments: Novel use of group-relative scores to convert absolute to relative advantages is elegant; scalability claims strong but limited to mathematical domains.

model_io.model: Self-evolving multimodal agent (Actor + Judge + GRPO)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-consistency signal enables stable self-evolution without external reward models or human annotations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; group relative policy optimization achieves improvements without supervision

title: CarePilot: A Multi-Agent Framework for Long-Horizon Computer Task Automation in Healthcare
task: Long-horizon computer task automation in healthcare systems
key_results: CarePilot outperforms closed-source/open-source baselines by ~15.26% and 3.38% on CareFlow benchmark
comments: Actor-critic with dual memory is well-motivated; CareFlow benchmark valuable for healthcare domain but limited scope (4 application types).

model_io.model: CarePilot (actor-critic multimodal agent)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dual-memory (long-term + short-term) with iterative refinement improves long-horizon medical workflow automation

key_results: SOTA on iPhone360, iPhone, DAVIS datasets; robust to 135° separation between training and test views
comments: 3D-native initialization via AnchorTAP3D is principled; iPhone360 benchmark is useful but relatively small scale.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 3D-native initialization mitigates 2D-native prior overfitting and improves occluded region consistency

comments: Physical condition generator and physics-enhanced decoder are well-motivated; CARLA simulation augmentation is clever but may introduce domain bias.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Learning from challenging trajectories in simulation improves physical consistency under counterfactual conditions

task: Inference-time mixed-precision quantization for video diffusion models

title: Optically driven control of mechanochemistry and fusion dynamics of biomolecular condensates via thymine dimerization

key_results: UV-induced thymine dimerization drives liquid-to-solid transitions and arrests droplet fusion; inter- versus intrachain crosslink balance determines mechanical state
comments: Novel optochemical control mechanism for condensates is elegant but functional relevance in cellular contexts remains speculative.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Photochemical crosslinking alters condensate nucleation and coalescence in a topology-dependent manner

title: Temperature-dependent gating pathways in TRPV3

comments: Supports heat capacity model for thermosensing but tetramer-to-pentamer transition suggests complexity beyond current Gibbs-Helmholtz framework.

model_io.model: Oxidized TRPV3 in lipid bilayer ± THCV

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cold and heat activation in TRPV3 follow symmetric thermosensitive pathways consistent with heat capacity model
hypotheses.result: Initial cold and heat activation share similar thermosensitivity despite distinct conformational states, supporting heat capacity model

hypotheses.hypothesis: THCV-induced inactivation is temperature-dependent and coupled to lipid release
hypotheses.result: Inactivation occurs only below 30°C; above 30°C, lipid release prevents inactivation and triggers unpredictable pore dilation with tetramer-to-pentamer transition

model_io.model: Custom 2-photon microscope and selected commercial systems

title: The NPC1/USP7/p53 axis regulates cholesterol and promotes the proliferation of hepatocellular carcinoma
task: Determine NPC1-USP7-p53 axis regulation of cholesterol metabolism and HCC proliferation
key_results: NPC1 upregulated in HCC, correlates with poor prognosis; NPC1 silencing suppresses proliferation in vitro/in vivo; NPC1 disrupts USP7-p53 interaction, enhancing p53 ubiquitination/degradation; modulates cholesterol via p53-SREBP2 axis
comments: Links lysosomal cholesterol transport to p53 stability and oncogenic metabolism; dual-targeting (NPC1 + p53 activation) may overcome metabolic adaptation.

model_io.model: HCC cells ± NPC1/USP7/p53 modulation; mouse xenograft models

hypotheses.hypothesis: NPC1 promotes HCC via USP7-mediated p53 destabilization and cholesterol modulation

title: The oncogenic control of nucleotide synthesis
task: Synthesize mechanisms of oncogenic control of nucleotide biosynthesis and salvage pathways

comments: Comprehensive review synthesizes environment-shaped routing model; therapeutic opportunities exist at DHODH, IMPDH and precursor availability nodes, particularly in combination with DNA-damaging therapies.

title: Moonlighting cytosolic function of ACAD9: suppression of TRAF6-mediated osteoclastogenesis and protection against osteoporosis

key_results: ACAD9 blocks TRAF6 K63-polyubiquitination and promotes K48-linked degradation; Acad9-KO mice show increased osteoclast numbers and decreased bone mass

hypotheses.hypothesis: ACAD9 suppresses osteoclastogenesis via TRAF6 inhibition

title: DCTPP1 orchestrates dCTP pool dynamics and mtDNA stability in quiescent cells
task: Characterize DCTPP1 function in mitochondrial dNTP pool dynamics and mtDNA stability during quiescence
key_results: DCTPP1 depletes dCTP/dGTP pools; predominantly mitochondrial during quiescence; DCTPP1 knockdown restores dCTP and mtDNA in thymidine-overloaded cells (MNGIE model)
comments: Identifies DCTPP1 as potential therapeutic target for mtDNA depletion syndromes; context-dependent (quiescence vs. replication) regulation suggests precision medicine approach.

hypotheses.hypothesis: DCTPP1 knockdown restores dCTP availability and mtDNA in MNGIE-like conditions

title: c-Myc transactivates CFL1 to induce senescence-like phenotype and potentiate the bystander effects for the migration and proliferation in lung cancer cells
task: Elucidate c-Myc transactivation of CFL1 in senescence-associated secretory phenotype (SASP) to promote bystander cell migration/proliferation

hypotheses.hypothesis: PS-NP exposure accelerates EC via AMPK-ACSS2-PLA2G3-AA pathway

model_io.model: Avian PGCs, HT-1080, PC-3, MDA-MB-231 extravasation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Extravasating cells use lineage-specific Ca²⁺ pathways despite conserved bleb mechanics

task: Elucidate KAT8 K145 lactylation mechanism in p53 acetylation and pro-apoptotic function

title: Role of dysregulated calcium homeostasis in astrocytes in neurodegenerative disorders

comments: Excellent efficiency-accuracy trade-off via ECFPN and channel pruning; practical for resource-constrained deployment.

model_io.model: Scale-Oriented Detection Network (SODNet)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Efficient context feature pyramid with multi-scale grouped fusion reduces computation while maintaining accuracy
hypotheses.result: Achieved 40.3 FPS deployment with minimal accuracy loss

title: Quasi-integrability from $$\mathcal{P}\mathcal{T}$$-symmetry
task: Quasi-integrability from PT-symmetry in deformed integrable systems
key_results: PT-symmetry is natural cause of quasi-integrability; asymptotic conservation of quasi-conserved charges follows from PT-symmetric phase; demonstrated for KdV, NLSE, non-local NLSE
comments: Theoretical result connecting PT-symmetry to quasi-integrability; Lax pair properties preserved under deformation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PT-symmetry explains quasi-integrability in deformed integrable models
hypotheses.result: Quasi-conserved charge densities acquire definite PT-properties enabling asymptotic conservation for KdV, NLSE, non-local NLSE

title: DNA affects the phenotype of fuel-dependent coacervate droplets
task: Establish genotype-phenotype relationship in DNA-containing coacervate synthetic cells
key_results: Guanine-rich DNA sequences kinetically trap droplets altering dissolution and morphology; adenine-rich sequences shorten lifetimes via hybridization; preferences for guanine/adenine-rich motifs revealed by sequencing
comments: Elegant demonstration of genotype effects in minimal protocells; limited to sequence preferences but establishes important design principles for synthetic biology.

model_io.model: fuel-dependent peptide-RNA coacervate droplets with DNA genotype library

hypotheses.hypothesis: DNA sequences partition preferentially into coacervate droplets based on sequence composition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; strong preferences for guanine-rich and adenine-rich motifs

hypotheses.hypothesis: DNA genotype affects synthetic cell phenotype through sequence-specific interactions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; different sequence classes modulate droplet lifetime and kinetics

title: Scalable foundation interatomic potentials via message-passing pruning and graph partitioning
task: Scale atomistic foundation models via message-passing pruning and graph partitioning for large-scale MD
key_results: Pruning removes low-contribution message-passing layers reducing parameters while preserving accuracy; GPU-distributed graph partitioning enables million-atom simulations at AFM-level accuracy on nanosecond timescales
comments: Practical engineering approach addressing AFM scalability bottlenecks; pruning methodology is general and results show strong speedup-accuracy trade-off.

model_io.model: pruned message-passing AFM with graph partitioning

hypotheses.hypothesis: Low-contribution message-passing layers can be removed without accuracy loss
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; significant parameter reduction while preserving data-efficiency

hypotheses.hypothesis: Graph-partitioned GPU distribution enables million-atom AFM simulations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; nanosecond timescale simulations achieved on single/multiple GPUs

title: Rational design of polymeric dielectrics guided by insightful understanding of electron transfer/transport in aperiodic systems
task: Rationally design polymeric dielectrics by controlling electron transfer and transport in aperiodic systems
key_results: Energy barrier and spatial confinement of frontier orbitals are pivotal in regulating electron transfer; ab-initio descriptors enable prediction and design of dielectric performance
comments: Novel quantitative real-space orbital approach to aperiodic condensed matter is valuable, though validation on single polymer system limits generalizability.

model_io.model: polymeric dielectric with frontier orbital engineering

hypotheses.hypothesis: Energy barrier and spatial confinement of unoccupied frontier orbitals regulate electron transfer
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; predictable and experimentally characterizable

hypotheses.hypothesis: Structure-dependent quantum current influences macroscopic conduction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; strong structure-property correlation demonstrated

title: Influence of web openings on the cyclic response of RC coupling beams
task: Assess cyclic response of RC coupling beams with web openings via finite element analysis
key_results: Mid-span opening with dimensions lo/l=0.125, ho/t=0.25 reduced shear capacity by 14.5-31.6% and max chord rotation by 48.8-65.6% depending on reinforcement; diagonal confinement least affected by openings
comments: Comprehensive parametric FE study but limited to single geometry; findings important for practical design but lack novel theoretical insights.

model_io.model: RC coupling beam (ABAQUS nonlinear FE)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Opening presence and location significantly alter cyclic behavior
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; mid-span location most detrimental

hypotheses.hypothesis: Diagonal confinement reinforcement is more robust to openings
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; diagonal confinement shows smallest capacity/rotation reductions

title: Exact and numerical optical soliton solutions of the fractional quadratic–cubic nonlinear Schrödinger equation in conformable derivative framework
task: Solve fractional quadratic-cubic NLSE with conformable derivative via analytical and numerical methods
key_results: Modified Sardar sub-equation and alternative methods yield new kink, bright, dark-bright, and singular wave solutions; differential transform method validates analytical solutions with high precision
comments: Comprehensive analytical-numerical study with good solution diversity, but limited novelty in methods applied; validation via DTM adds credibility.

model_io.model: fractional quadratic-cubic NLSE with conformable derivative

hypotheses.hypothesis: Modified Sardar and alternative methods yield new solutions for fractional NLSE
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; new kink, bright, periodic, dark-bright, singular solutions identified

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fractional parameter variation significantly affects solution behavior
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; detailed effect analysis via 3D and 2D graphs

title: Influence of programming-pulse properties on weight-update characteristics of charge-trapping IGZO synaptic transistors
task: Optimize programming-pulse width for weight-update in IGZO synaptic transistors
key_results: Dynamic ratio peaks at PPW=3s (18.5) compared to 1.5s and 6s; optimal PPW balances energy per pulse and recovery kinetics during read operations
comments: Device-level optimization shows clear trade-offs but conclusions limited to specific IGZO architecture; AI accelerator simulation validation is appreciated.

model_io.model: charge-trapping a-IGZO TFT synapse

hypotheses.hypothesis: Programming pulse width critically affects weight-update characteristics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; PPW=3s yields optimal dynamic ratio of 18.5

hypotheses.hypothesis: Recovery during read degrades weight retention for longer PPWs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; faster recovery at PPW=6s reduces dynamic ratio

title: A novel filtering T-shaped transmission line structure and its application in the design of reduced-size Butler matrix
task: Design miniaturized filtering Butler matrix using novel T-shaped transmission line
key_results: T-shaped structure behaves as λ/4 line with tunable frequency rejection; 4×4 Butler matrix reduced 65% in size with integrated low-pass filtering; measured results agree with theory and simulation
comments: Clever structural innovation combining size reduction with filtering; practical design with excellent agreement between theory, simulation, and measurement.

model_io.model: T-shaped transmission line and Butler matrix

hypotheses.hypothesis: T-shaped structure functions as λ/4 line at specific frequency while providing frequency rejection
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; filtering behavior integrated into structure

hypotheses.hypothesis: Butler matrix miniaturization does not significantly degrade beamforming performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ±40° steering angle and 10 dBi gain achieved

title: Structural, optical and luminescence properties of Fe3+-doped mixed alkali zirconia-borate glasses for warm orange-red photonic applications
task: Engineer Fe3+-doped mixed alkali zirconia-borate glasses for warm orange-red photonics
key_results: Bandgap reduced from 3.90 to 3.10 eV (direct) by Fe3+ doping; photoluminescence orange-red emission at 550-570 nm with CIE chromaticity suitable for warm-emitting applications
comments: Thorough structural and optical characterization but application to actual photonic devices not demonstrated; materials science work is solid.

model_io.model: Fe3+-doped mixed alkali zirconia-borate glass

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fe3+ doping introduces in-gap states narrowing bandgap
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; bandgap reduced from 3.90 to 3.10 eV

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mixed alkali zirconia-borate host enables orange-red emission suitable for warm photonics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CIE values indicate warm-emitting compatibility

title: Suppression of hysteresis in ultrathin tellurium transistors
task: Suppress electrical hysteresis in ultrathin tellurium transistors via dielectric encapsulation and dual-gate architecture
key_results: Al2O3 encapsulation reduces gas-induced hysteresis and improves mobility to ~80 cm² V⁻¹ s⁻¹ with ION/IOFF>10⁵; dual-gate achieves hysteresis <1 V across wide sweep rates
comments: Elegant systematic approach identifying and mitigating multiple hysteresis sources; dual-gate solution is practical and BEOL-compatible.

model_io.model: Te FET with variable encapsulation
inputs: gate voltage sweep range and rate

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gas molecule reorientation causes large hysteresis in exposed devices
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; encapsulation suppresses gas-induced hysteresis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dual-gate architecture stabilizes channel electrostatics against charge trapping
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; residual hysteresis reduced below 1 V

title: Adaptive governance in collective risk social dilemmas
task: Optimize adaptive governance for collective risk dilemmas
key_results: Dynamically adjusted targets increase cooperation more than static targets; moderate target increases effective at low risk, higher targets sustain cooperation at high risk
comments: Applies game theory to social cooperation but lacks empirical validation beyond theoretical modeling.

model_io.model: repeated game with dynamic governance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adjusting collective targets promotes cooperation more than static governance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; target adjustment crucial for high cooperation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reward intensity effects differ by risk level
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; rewards expand cooperation basin at low risk but have little effect at high risk

title: High-entropy design of transition metal oxide semiconductors with ultra-low thermal conductivity
task: Engineer semiconducting metal oxides with ultra-low thermal conductivity via high-entropy design
key_results: Achieved thermal conductivity <1 W/m/K in high-entropy wolframite A6WO4; bandgap narrowing from 3.90 to 3.10 eV (direct) enabled by cation mixing
comments: Demonstrates elegant mechanism coupling electronic and phonon engineering through entropy, with clear experimental validation on a single material system.

model_io.model: high-entropy wolframite oxide (A=Mn,Fe,Co,Ni,Cu,Zn)

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-entropy cation mixing induces semiconducting behavior in insulating oxides
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; configurational entropy drives transition with reduced bandgap

hypotheses.hypothesis: Chemical disorder reduces thermal conductivity via phonon scattering
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; phonon-defect and three-phonon scattering dominate

title: Achieving wideband wavefront manipulation in asymmetric media by phase-engineered metasurfaces with near-unity transmission
task: Achieve wideband wavefront manipulation in asymmetric media via phase-engineered metasurfaces
key_results: Cascaded gradient Huygens' metasurface achieves ~13% relative bandwidth (X-band) with near-unity transmission and 2π phase coverage; demonstrated beam steering and energy focusing
comments: Clever use of dielectric spacers as design degree of freedom; experimental validation and practical applications strengthen contribution.

model_io.model: cascaded phase-engineered gradient Huygens' metasurface

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dielectric spacers optimize resonance and propagation phases for wideband impedance matching
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 13% relative bandwidth achieved with near-unity transmission

hypotheses.hypothesis: Strategy is scalable to multiple frequency bands and asymmetric systems
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; demonstrated in X-band with potential for nondestructive testing and sensing
